title: Add edge resize support for group selection
description: Remove isSingleSelection guards that prevent edge resize handles on group selections. The resize math in handleResize already handles groups with proportional scaling. Changes needed in SelectionBoxView.swift (edge handle rendering) and SelectionBox.swift (edge hit-testing). Low complexity.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add label editing support for line/arrow objects
description: Implement double-click on midpoint to add/edit label text on line/arrow. Label renders at midpoint with TextAttributes. isEditingLabel flag controls edit mode. Label area included in hit testing. Reference: NODE_ARCHITECTURE_PROPOSAL.md Phase 1, ARCHITECTURE.md section 3.3
notes: Day 3-4: Detect double-click on line/arrow midPoint. Set isEditingLabel=true, show TextField at midPoint. User types label text, stored in label property. Press Enter/click away to commit. Label renders at midPoint in LineObjectView/ArrowObjectView.

title: Update Views/CanvasView.swift: single ForEach for shapes, preset-driven drag preview, isShapeTool checks, addShape() on drag end
close_reason: CanvasView updated: single ForEach for shapeObjects, preset-driven drag preview, isShapeTool checks, addShape() on drag end

title: Phase 2.1: Create selection box with resize handles
description:
Build SelectionBoxView:
- 4 corner handles for diagonal resize
- 4 edge handles for horizontal/vertical resize
- Combined bounding box for multi-selection
- Hit testing for handles
close_reason: Implemented SelectionBoxView with corner/edge resize handles, rotation handles, combined bounding box for multi-selection, and hit testing. Added SelectionBox model, SelectionBoxView, ResizeHandle components, and unit tests.

title: Implement LineObject data model
description: Create LineObject.swift conforming to CanvasObject and StrokableObject protocols. Includes two control points (startPoint/endPoint), stroke properties, optional label support, and hit testing for control points, line segment, and label area. Reference: NODE_ARCHITECTURE_PROPOSAL.md Phase 1, ARCHITECTURE.md section 3.3
notes: Day 1: Data model implementation. Define struct with id, startPoint, endPoint, stroke properties, label support. Implement CanvasObject protocol conformance with computed position/size/midPoint properties. Implement hit testing for control points, line segment (using point-to-line-segment distance), and label area.

title: Phase 3.2: Implement Arrow tool
description:
Create arrow tool:
- ArrowObject extending line
- Arrow head styles (none, open, filled, circle, diamond)
- Arrow at start/end/both

title: Implement copy, cut, and paste for canvas objects
description:
Support clipboard operations for canvas objects:
- Cmd+C to copy selected object(s)
- Cmd+X to cut selected object(s)
- Cmd+V to paste copied object(s) with slight offset
- Works for text, rectangle, and circle objects
- Multi-selection copy/paste if group selection exists
close_reason: Closed

title: Add rotation support for group selection
description: Enable rotation for group/multi-selection. 1) Show rotation handles for groups (remove isSingleSelection guards). 2) Fix rotation center to use group bounding box center. 3) Implement orbit math: each object position orbits group center by delta angle, individual rotation incremented by delta. 4) Keep group box axis-aligned. After rotation, each object has independent position/rotation — no persistent group state.
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 6.1: Implement keyboard shortcuts
description:
Add shortcuts:
- Tool switching (V, H, R, L, etc.)
- Delete, duplicate, select all
- Arrow keys for nudge

title: Implement two-point drag-to-create gesture for line/arrow
description: Implement drag gesture handling in CanvasView/GestureCoordinator for .line and .arrow tools. User drags from start point to end point to create line/arrow. Show preview during drag. Create object on drag end. Reference: NODE_ARCHITECTURE_PROPOSAL.md Phase 1 Day 3-4
notes: Day 3-4: In GestureCoordinator/CanvasView, handle drag gesture for line/arrow tools. On drag start, capture startPoint. On drag changed, update preview with currentPoint. On drag end, create LineObject or ArrowObject with final startPoint/endPoint. Add to objects array. Show visual preview during drag.

title: Phase 2.2: Implement resize functionality
description:
Add object resizing:
- Corner drag for diagonal resize
- Edge drag for single-axis resize
- Shift+drag for proportional

close_reason: Closed

notes: Day 1: Extends LineObject pattern with ArrowHead enum. Add startArrowHead/endArrowHead properties. Same computed properties and hit testing as LineObject. Should be straightforward after LineObject is complete.

title: Phase 6.2: Implement undo/redo system

title: Design and document node architecture enhancements
description: Evaluate and design optional scene graph architecture enhancements for TextTool. Analyze whether hierarchical grouping, transform inheritance, spatial indexing, or connection system are needed based on current product requirements and performance constraints. Document decision rationale and implementation approach if approved. Reference: NODE_ARCHITECTURE_PROPOSAL.md sections on Optional Scene Graph Enhancements.
close_reason: Architecture evaluation complete. Conclusion: Keep flat array architecture (already production-ready). Scene graph not needed for current annotation use case. All design documents created: ARCHITECTURE_ANALYSIS.md, SCENE_GRAPH_EVALUATION.md, HIERARCHICAL_GROUPING_DESIGN.md, SPATIAL_INDEXING_DESIGN.md, NODE_ARCHITECTURE_PROPOSAL.md. Will reconsider scene graph only if performance degrades or users request hierarchical features.

title: Analyze current architecture limitations and performance
description: Analyze current flat array architecture for canvas objects. Measure performance with varying object counts (10, 50, 100, 200 objects). Identify bottlenecks: hit testing O(n), rendering, selection box calculation. Document current limitations: no hierarchical grouping, no transform inheritance, manual group management. Determine if scene graph is needed or if current architecture is sufficient.
close_reason: Closed

title: Evaluate scene graph vs flat array trade-offs
description: Compare scene graph (hierarchical tree) vs current flat array approach. Analyze trade-offs: complexity vs capabilities, performance at scale, memory usage, development time. Consider TextTool's current use cases (annotation tool) vs potential future needs (diagram features). Document recommendation: keep flat array, migrate to scene graph, or hybrid approach. Reference: NODE_ARCHITECTURE_PROPOSAL.md Optional Scene Graph Enhancements section.
close_reason: Closed

title: Design hierarchical grouping system (if needed)
description: Design GroupNode system for parent-child relationships. Define how groups contain children (UUID references), manage transform inheritance (position/rotation/scale propagate to children), handle group selection/manipulation. Design API: addNode(parent:), removeNode(), getChildren(), getAncestors(). Specify how this integrates with existing AnyCanvasObject system. Reference: NODE_ARCHITECTURE_PROPOSAL.md SceneGraph and GroupNode sections.
close_reason: Completed hierarchical grouping system design. Created HIERARCHICAL_GROUPING_DESIGN.md with complete architecture: GroupNode protocol, SceneGraph manager, transform inheritance, API design, selection behavior, integration strategy, and testing plan. Design is conditional - implementation deferred until triggers are met per SCENE_GRAPH_EVALUATION.md.

title: Design spatial indexing system (if needed)
description: Design QuadTree or R-tree spatial indexing for O(log n) hit testing. Define structure: QuadTree<UUID> storing object IDs in spatial buckets, rebuild on object move/resize. Design API: query(point), query(rect), insert(id, bounds), remove(id). Specify integration with CanvasViewModel: when to rebuild index, how to keep in sync with object array. Measure expected performance improvement. Reference: NODE_ARCHITECTURE_PROPOSAL.md Spatial Indexing section.
close_reason: Closed

title: Design connection/edge system architecture (if needed)
description: Design system for semantic connections between objects (React Flow pattern). Define Connection struct with source/target IDs, handle positions, routing type. Design NodeHandle for connection points on objects. Specify how connections update when objects move (automatic position recalculation). Consider: is this needed for annotation tool, or defer until diagram features requested? Reference: NODE_ARCHITECTURE_PROPOSAL.md Connection System section.
close_reason: Closed

title: Prototype SceneGraph core implementation
description: Create proof-of-concept SceneGraph class with node storage ([UUID: AnySceneNode]), hierarchy tracking (parent/children relationships), rendering order computation (depth-first with zIndex). Implement addNode(), removeNode(), getNode(), getRenderOrder(). Test with existing ShapeObject and TextObject types. Verify performance improvement over flat array. Reference: NODE_ARCHITECTURE_PROPOSAL.md SceneGraph Structure section.
close_reason: Scene graph not needed per SCENE_GRAPH_EVALUATION.md - flat array architecture is sufficient for current annotation use case. Will reconsider only if: (1) performance degrades, (2) object counts exceed 100 regularly, or (3) users request hierarchical grouping features.

title: Design migration strategy from flat array to scene graph
description: Plan incremental migration from objects: [AnyCanvasObject] to sceneGraph: SceneGraph. Design dual-mode approach: support both systems during transition with sync layer. Define migration phases: Phase 1 (add SceneGraph, keep array), Phase 2 (migrate hit testing), Phase 3 (migrate rendering), Phase 4 (remove array). Specify backward compatibility requirements and testing strategy. Reference: NODE_ARCHITECTURE_PROPOSAL.md Migration Strategy section.
close_reason: Scene graph not needed per SCENE_GRAPH_EVALUATION.md - flat array architecture is sufficient for current annotation use case. Will reconsider only if: (1) performance degrades, (2) object counts exceed 100 regularly, or (3) users request hierarchical grouping features.

title: Document node architecture decision and roadmap
description: Document final decision on node architecture enhancements: which features to implement (scene graph, grouping, spatial indexing, connections) and which to defer. Create implementation roadmap with phases, timeline, resource requirements. Update ARCHITECTURE.md with decision rationale and chosen approach. Provide go/no-go recommendation for each optional enhancement based on analysis. Reference: all NODE_ARCHITECTURE_PROPOSAL.md sections.
close_reason: Scene graph not needed per SCENE_GRAPH_EVALUATION.md - flat array architecture is sufficient for current annotation use case. Will reconsider only if: (1) performance degrades, (2) object counts exceed 100 regularly, or (3) users request hierarchical grouping features.
